description_long: This kiosk is a monolithic stone structure mounted on a square stone base, featuring intricately carved human faces on each of its four sides. The upper portion is cylindrical and capped, with elaborate headdresses and stylized hair details framing each face, suggesting a cultural or religious significance. The stone surface is dark gray, with a visibly rough, matte finish and subtle chisel marks indicating hand-carved craftsmanship. A rectangular plaque is affixed to the front of the base, likely for informational or commemorative purposes. The kiosk lacks any visible openings, windows, or doors, and its solid construction implies it is not meant for entry or interior use. The overall design is reminiscent of ancient or traditional architectural motifs, with a focus on sculptural ornamentation and symbolic representation.
description: A solid stone kiosk with carved human faces on all sides, mounted on a square base and featuring a capped cylindrical top. The surface is dark gray, rough, and intricately detailed, with a plaque on the front.
description_view_0: Front view shows the most prominent carved face and a rectangular plaque on the base.
description_view_1: This view highlights another carved face with a different headdress, and the plaque is partially visible on the left.
description_view_2: The rear face is visible, featuring a distinct facial expression and headdress, with no plaque in sight.
description_view_3: Another side face is shown, with the plaque just visible on the right edge of the base.
attribute_signage_type: printed sign, rectangular plaque affixed to the front base, no visible text
attribute_roof_design: cylindrical capped top, solid stone, no overhangs or decorative roof features
attribute_architectural_style: traditional, sculptural, with ancient or religious influences
